title: pomaderris clivicola
intro: Pomaderris clivicola, a shrub native to eastern Australia, is a member of the Rhamnaceae family and is commonly known as the sandpaper tree due to its rough bark. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Pomaderris clivicola include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used by indigenous Australians for generations to harness its medicinal properties.
